cards.back: A supervised learning algorithm where the algorithm makes predictions based on the most similar examples in the training set.

cards.front: What is the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm?
cards.back: A variation of the k-Nearest-Neighbor algorithm where the distance between the instance and the training data is weighted according to its importance.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the weight function in the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm?
cards.back: To give more importance to the closest instances and less importance to the farther instances in the training set.

cards.front: What is the main advantage of the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm?
cards.back: It can handle cases where some instances in the training set are more relevant than others.

cards.front: What is the main disadvantage of the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm?
cards.back: It can be computationally expensive and may be prone to overfitting.
